text.title: 14 Days in Japan 🇯🇵 (Biking Mt.Fuji, Jazz in Tokyo, Matcha Mochi, + 4 Unique Cities)

JY 25.
The doors on the right side will open.

right there.
We made it to Japan.
Lauren's using the bathroom right now.
We got our bags.

Now we got to catch our first train to

What do you want to do?

Thank you for joining me.

Osaka.
You got to appreciate
soccer castle.
So, the interesting thing about the
Osaka Castle is that it's actually not
the original. And that's the same for a
lot of historic castles in Japan cuz
either they're struck by lightning or a
war demolishes the castle. And in this
instance, both happened. So, this is a
1930s reproduction of the original made
out of concrete, which is really, really
cool. Hey
baby.
Man, do I love Japan.

Good morning.
>> One of the things that I've learned
about being in Osaka compared to other
parts of Japan is that Osaka is a really
unique experience in Japan. actually
like I was kind of doubting whether or
not it would be that, but it is like
this tower.
It's so cool.
But beyond just like the monuments and
the things you can see, the people here
are actually very different in essence
and very different in character than
other parts of Japan. It kind of reminds
me of Cojima or Okayama where you know
in the city everyone's hustle and bustle
and a bit tough, but as you move away
from Tokyo in specific, you start to see
the character or the playfulness of
Japanese people, and we definitely
experience that in Osaka. Not to say
that Tokyo doesn't have it either, cuz
I'm a big city guy. Obviously, I live in
New York City. New York City has a ton
of character, but Osaka has something
that uh is special in a lot of regards.
So, it's kind of unique to experience
it. And it's something that will stay
with me for the rest of my life. That's
for sure.
>> Today, we're going to Kyoto. And as
always, we have a train to catch.
sunny sunny Kyoko.

just told Lauren, it's like a fish in
water. Like a fish in water doesn't
realize how special it is. it that it
can swim, that it has gills, that it can
do all these things in the water. But if
a bird dives or dove head first into
water, it would know right away. And it
would experience a different sensation
than a fish would. And that's the way we
feel. We feel like birds in water. And

One of the biggest differences between
riding trains in Japan versus riding
trains in America, New York City really
to be spec to be specific. I guess we've
ridden trains in Boston and and DC as
well, but New York City is the
efficiency. So the trains are almost
always on time here, which is like can't
ask for more. And even if you miss the
train, for whatever reason, you miss it.
There will always be another one that
will come 4, 3, 5 minutes after. So it
doesn't really mean you lose that much
time by missing the train. The other
thing I'll say is the cleanliness here
is like it's just on another level. You
just don't get as clean of environments
in the US and I would say in the
European countries that I visited. It's
just on another level. I'm curious to
know if I wonder if places like in uh

great great January, which is when I'm
recording this. If it's another month,

and you're watching this, shout out to
you. I appreciate it. and we'll see you
in the next one.
Peace, guys.
